title: Seeded Recursive Backtracking Generation · Runtime Lens: Seed Ownership
summary: Generation chooses visited neighbors, recursive depth overflows silently, random state is global, and completion is confused with connectivity. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the seed ownership increment in seeded iterative and recursive backtracker. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inject and record a dedicated random generator and seed without contaminating global random state.
Enumerate in-bounds unvisited neighbors before randomized selection and retain deterministic ordering before shuffling.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example puzzle team handles case 6271 during a keyboard-only puzzle tournament. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate seeded backtracking progress, completion, topology, depth, and replay; resource use must remain below an explicit budget; then inject one invalid wall, stale callback, or corrupted field and reject it. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Inject and record a dedicated random generator and seed without contaminating global random state. A defensible solution applies Seed Ownership, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to seeded iterative and recursive backtracker.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-generation-backtracker-2
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-generation-backtracker-2.
# Case 7417: a keyboard-only puzzle tournament.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant.
def worked_maze_generation_backtracker_2(cells: int = 48, visited: int = 48, carved_edges: int = 47, stack_peak: int = 21, replay_match: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "gridworked2",
        "topology7417",
        "search2dekrow",
        "eventworked27417",
        "access7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if cells < 1 or visited < 0 or carved_edges < 0:
        return False, visited, "generation-count"
    if visited != cells:
        return False, visited, "unvisited-cell"
    if carved_edges != cells - 1:
        return False, carved_edges, "tree-shape"
    if stack_peak < 1 or not replay_match:
        return False, stack_peak, "replay-invariant"
    assert visited == cells and carved_edges == cells - 1
    return True, visited, "seeded-backtracker"

# Evidence: maze-seed-ownership-1fn5rd
# Competency: maze-seed-ownership.

def evidence_maze_seed_ownership_1fn5rd(cells: int = 48, visited: int = 48, carved_edges: int = 47, stack_peak: int = 21, replay_match: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:

        "grid1fn5rd",
        "topology2633",
        "searchdr5nf1",
        "event1fn5rd2633",

    if visited != cells:

    if carved_edges != cells - 1:
        return False, carved_edges, "tree-shape"
    if stack_peak < 1 or not replay_match:
        return False, stack_peak, "replay-invariant"
    assert visited == cells and carved_edges == cells - 1
    return True, visited, "seeded-backtracker"

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Inject and record a dedicated random generator and seed without contaminating global random state.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Seed Ownership model · Theory Seed Ownership, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 4461 during a corrupted-state recovery drill. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate seeded backtracking progress, completion, topology, depth, and replay; the prior good state must survive failure; then change the seed and retain replay identity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Inject and record a dedicated random generator and seed without contaminating global random state.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Seed Ownership model · Theory Seed Ownership, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Calling seed once makes every future run independently reproducible.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: maze-seed-ownership-1fn5rd.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: maze-seed-ownership-1fn5rd(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_maze_seed_ownership_1fn5rd\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?cells < 1 or visited < 0 or carved_edges < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?visited != cells)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?carved_edges != cells - 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?stack_peak < 1 or not replay_match)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
